title: Custom Themes
requirements: BootMii and Priiloader already installed (Wii — strongly recommended brick protection for this)
A Windows PC (or Wine on Mac) to build the theme file with New ThemeMii MOD
warnings: !!Installing a theme from the wrong System Menu version or region can brick your Wii!!. Always match the base theme you download to your own console's version and region exactly.
Make sure BootMii and Priiloader are installed before doing this — they're your recovery path if a theme install goes wrong.
_note: csm-installer runs on the Wii itself (via the Homebrew Channel) and installs finished .csm theme files. New ThemeMii MOD is the PC-side tool that builds a .csm file from a .mym theme plus your console's base theme — it isn't something you run on the Wii, so it isn't copied to your SD card automatically.

steps.title: Before you start
steps.body:
Custom themes replace the look of your Wii Menu. The process has two halves: building a `.csm` theme file on a computer with **New ThemeMii MOD**, then installing it on the console with **csm-installer**.

!!Version and region matching matters!! — a theme built for the wrong System Menu version or region can brick your Wii Menu. Having **BootMii** and **Priiloader** installed first means you can recover if that happens.

First, I'll check your SD card is FAT32.

steps.title: Put csm-installer on your SD card
steps.body: Graphite downloads **csm-installer** and unpacks it into the `apps` folder on your SD card. This is the on-console app that grabs your original theme and later installs your custom one.

steps.title: Clean up and eject the card
steps.body:
macOS scatters hidden junk files (`.DS_Store`, `._` files) onto SD cards, and Wii homebrew can choke on them.

Press the button, and Graphite strips those files off the card and **safely ejects it for you**. Once it pops off, pull it out.

steps.title: Download your original base theme
steps.body:
Put the SD card in your Wii and power it on. Launch the **Homebrew Channel** and open **csm-installer**.

Select **Download original Wii theme (Base theme)**. This saves a copy of your console's unmodified theme to the SD card — New ThemeMii MOD needs this exact file, matching your version and region, to build a working custom theme.

When it's done, power off the Wii and move the SD card back to your computer.

troubleshoot.label: csm-installer can't find or download the base theme

steps.title: Build your custom theme
steps.body:
On a Windows PC (or via Wine on Mac), download **New ThemeMii MOD** from [wii.hacks.guide/assets/files/New_ThemeMii_MOD.zip](https://wii.hacks.guide/assets/files/New_ThemeMii_MOD.zip) and extract it.

1. Get a `.mym` theme file from a community source (GBAtemp forums, Wii Themer, or similar) — make sure it matches your console's version and region.
2. Open **New ThemeMii MOD**, select your `.mym` file, and point it at the **base theme** you just copied off your SD card.
3. Build the theme — this produces a `.csm` file.
4. Copy that `.csm` file onto your SD card's root (or wherever csm-installer expects it — check the app's on-screen instructions).

steps.title: Install the theme
steps.body:
Put the SD card back in your Wii, launch the **Homebrew Channel**, and open **csm-installer** again.

Select your `.csm` file from the list and confirm the install. !!Double check the version/region match one more time before confirming!! — this is the step that can brick the System Menu if it doesn't match.

Once it finishes, reboot to the System Menu to see your new theme.

troubleshoot.label: The theme install failed or looks wrong

steps.title: You're done
steps.body: Your Wii Menu now uses your custom theme. If anything looks broken or the console won't boot normally, use Priiloader (hold **RESET** while powering on) to get back into a working menu, or restore your NAND backup with BootMii as a last resort.
